name: Electronic Protected Health Information
description: Identifiable health information in electronic form under HIPAA.

name: Health Data (General)
description: General patient health data including diagnoses, treatment, and care journey records.
notes: Base category used by applicability engine for jurisdiction overlays.

name: 42 CFR Part 2 Substance Use Treatment Records
description: Records linked to federally assisted substance use disorder treatment programs.

name: Mental Health Records
description: Psychiatric, psychological, and behavioral care documentation.
notes: Often subject to enhanced consent and disclosure restrictions.

name: Reproductive Health Data
description: Fertility, pregnancy, abortion, and reproductive care information.

name: Pediatric Health Data
description: Health information related to minors, including parental/guardian governance context.

name: Genetic Data
description: Genome and hereditary data associated with an identifiable person.
notes: Cross-border transfer and consent constraints can be stricter than baseline PHI.

name: Clinical Trial Data
description: Research subject data used in regulated clinical studies.
notes: Subject to research governance and ethics oversight.

name: Genomic/Biobank Data
description: Large-scale genomic and biospecimen-linked datasets for precision medicine and research.
notes: Re-identification risk remains high despite pseudonymization.

name: Medical Device Data
description: Patient-linked telemetry and event data generated by regulated medical devices.
notes: Can be safety-critical and requires lifecycle security management.

name: EHDS Primary Use Data
description: Patient and clinician access/use context for European Health Data Space primary use.
notes: Governed by EU access and interoperability expectations.

name: EHDS Secondary Use Data
description: Health data reused for research, policy, and innovation under EHDS governance.
notes: Requires permit/opt-out handling and strict pseudonymization controls.

name: Imaging Data
description: Radiology and diagnostic image data (DICOM) with embedded metadata identifiers.
notes: DICOM headers may leak PHI even if pixel data is anonymized.

name: Prescription Data
description: Medication prescribing, dispensing, and controlled substance records.
notes: Fraud and diversion risk is high in e-prescribing workflows.

name: Clinical Operational Metadata
description: Scheduling, workflow, and operational data tied to treatment delivery.
notes: Can enable inference attacks even with partial de-identification.
